name: Physical Activity
definition: Bodily movement produced by skeletal muscles that requires energy expenditure. Includes occupational, transport, household, and leisure-time activity. Measured by duration, intensity (e.g., METs), and frequency. Physical activity improves cardiorespiratory fitness, insulin sensitivity, and mental health; reduces risk of obesity, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and some cancers. Sedentary behavior is a related but distinct exposure. Genetic variation in fitness and motivation may modify response to activity interventions.

systems_affected.mechanism_summary: Improves endothelial function, blood pressure, lipid profile; reduces CAD and stroke risk
Enhances insulin sensitivity; reduces T2D and obesity risk
Maintains bone density and muscle mass

sensitive_windows.mechanism_rationale: Establishes lifelong habits; bone and muscle development

references.title: Gene–physical activity interactions in obesity and type 2 diabetes
references.journal: Progress in Molecular Biology and Translational Science
